samples.query: Does the PPO Gold plan cover root canals?
samples.expected_answer: Yes, the PPO Gold plan covers root canal therapy under Basic Services at 80% after deductible. This includes anterior (D3310), premolar (D3320), and molar (D3330) root canals.
samples.relevant_context: Basic Services (80% after deductible): D3310 Root canal therapy (anterior), D3320 Root canal therapy (premolar), D3330 Root canal therapy (molar)

samples.query: What is the annual maximum for the Silver plan?
samples.expected_answer: The Delta Dental PPO Silver Plan has an annual maximum of $1,500 per member.
samples.relevant_context: Annual Maximum: $1,500 per member

samples.query: How much is the deductible for the HMO plan?
samples.expected_answer: The Delta Dental HMO Basic Plan has no deductible. It is a copay-based plan with fixed copays per procedure.
samples.relevant_context: Deductible: None. Copay-based plan: Fixed copays per procedure.

samples.query: Are orthodontic services covered under the Silver plan?
samples.expected_answer: No, orthodontic services are not included in the Silver plan. You would need to upgrade to the Gold plan for orthodontic coverage.
samples.relevant_context: Orthodontic Services (Not Covered): Orthodontic services are not included in the Silver plan. Upgrade to the Gold plan for orthodontic coverage.

samples.query: What is the copay for a crown on the HMO plan?
samples.expected_answer: On the HMO Basic Plan, the copay for a porcelain/ceramic crown (D2740) is $300, and for a porcelain fused to metal crown (D2750) it is $275. Crowns are limited to 1x per tooth per 5 years.
samples.relevant_context: D2740 Crown, porcelain/ceramic: $300 copay (1x per tooth per 5 years). D2750 Crown, porcelain fused to metal: $275 copay.

samples.query: How often can I get my teeth cleaned?
samples.expected_answer: Most plans cover two prophylaxis (cleaning) appointments per calendar year. This includes both adult cleanings (D1110) and child cleanings (D1120).
samples.relevant_context: D1110 Adult prophylaxis (cleaning): Covered 2x per calendar year. D1120 Child prophylaxis: Covered 2x per calendar year.

samples.query: What are the waiting periods for the PPO Gold plan?
samples.expected_answer: The PPO Gold plan has no waiting periods for Preventive & Diagnostic or Basic Services. Major Services and Orthodontic Services both have a 12-month waiting period from the plan effective date.
samples.relevant_context: Waiting Periods: Preventive & Diagnostic: None. Basic Services: None. Major Services: 12 months. Orthodontic Services: 12 months.

samples.query: Compare the PPO Gold and PPO Silver plans
samples.expected_answer: The PPO Gold has a higher annual maximum ($2,000 vs $1,500), lower deductible ($50/$150 vs $75/$225), better Basic Services coverage (80% vs 70%), better Major Services coverage (50% vs 40%), and includes orthodontic coverage (50% with $1,500 lifetime max). The Silver plan does not cover orthodontics.
samples.relevant_context: Gold: Annual Maximum $2,000, Deductible $50/150, Basic 80%, Major 50%, Ortho 50%. Silver: Annual Maximum $1,500, Deductible $75/225, Basic 70%, Major 40%, Ortho Not Covered.

samples.query: How do I file a dental claim?
samples.expected_answer: In-network dentists file claims electronically on your behalf. For out-of-network dentists, request an itemized statement and submit a claim form to Delta Dental within 12 months of service.
samples.relevant_context: In-network dentists file claims electronically on your behalf. Out-of-network: Request an itemized statement and submit a claim form to Delta Dental.

samples.query: Why was my claim denied?
samples.expected_answer: Common denial reasons include: procedure not covered under your plan, waiting period not met, annual maximum reached, frequency limitations exceeded, pre-authorization not obtained, or missing/incorrect claim information.
samples.relevant_context: Common reasons for claim denials include: procedure not covered, waiting period not met, annual maximum reached, frequency limitations exceeded, pre-authorization not obtained, missing or incorrect information.

samples.query: How many claims were denied?
samples.expected_answer: Based on the claims data analysis, I can provide the count of denied claims from the system.
samples.relevant_context: Claims summary data with status breakdown

samples.query: What are the most common dental procedures in the claims data?
samples.expected_answer: Based on the claims data, I can break down the most frequently billed procedure categories and specific CDT codes.
samples.relevant_context: Claims category breakdown data

samples.query: Are there any billing anomalies in the claims data?
samples.expected_answer: The anomaly detection system identifies claims where billed amounts significantly exceed the typical range for that procedure code.
samples.relevant_context: Anomaly detection results with specific claim IDs and deviation data

samples.query: What is the average cost for a root canal?
samples.expected_answer: Based on the claims data, I can provide the average billed and paid amounts for root canal procedures (D3310, D3320, D3330).
samples.relevant_context: Claims data for endodontic procedures

samples.query: What is the approval rate for dental claims?
samples.expected_answer: Based on the claims data analysis, I can provide the overall approval rate as a percentage of total claims.
samples.relevant_context: Claims summary with approval rate

samples.query: Are dental implants covered?
samples.expected_answer: Standard Delta Dental plans do not cover dental implants. However, some plans offer a Major Services rider that includes implant coverage. Contact your benefits administrator to check availability.
samples.relevant_context: Standard plans do not cover dental implants. Some plans offer a Major Services rider that includes implant coverage.

samples.query: What is pre-authorization?
samples.expected_answer: Pre-authorization is a review of proposed dental treatment before it is performed. Your dentist submits a treatment plan, and Delta Dental confirms coverage and payment level. It is recommended for major services exceeding $300.
samples.relevant_context: Pre-authorization is a review of proposed dental treatment before it is performed. Recommended for major services exceeding $300.

samples.query: Do I need a referral to see a specialist?
samples.expected_answer: It depends on your plan type. PPO plans do not require referrals — you can see any specialist directly. HMO plans require your primary care dentist to refer you to an in-network specialist.
samples.relevant_context: PPO plans: No referral needed. HMO plans: Yes, primary care dentist must refer you to an in-network specialist.

samples.query: What is the difference between PPO and HMO dental plans?
samples.expected_answer: PPO plans offer a network of dentists at reduced rates, allow out-of-network visits, and use deductibles/coinsurance. HMO plans require choosing a primary care dentist, use fixed copays with no deductibles, and typically don't cover out-of-network care except emergencies.
samples.relevant_context: PPO: Network of dentists, any dentist allowed, deductibles and coinsurance. HMO: Assigned primary care dentist, fixed copays, no deductibles, no out-of-network benefits.

samples.query: What happens if I exceed my annual maximum?
samples.expected_answer: Once you reach your annual maximum, you are responsible for 100% of additional dental costs for the rest of the plan year. The maximum resets on January 1st. Consider scheduling non-urgent procedures in the next plan year if approaching the limit.
samples.relevant_context: Once you reach your annual maximum, you are responsible for 100% of remaining dental costs. Maximum resets on January 1st.
